name: delegate_output_nonempty
code:
proof fn phi_1_delegate_output_nonempty(pre: AbstractHostState, post: AbstractHostState, pkt: Packet, out: Set<Packet>)
    requires
        pkt.msg is Message,
        pre.delegation_map.is_complete(),
        next_delegate(pre, post, pkt, out),
    ensures
        !(out =~= Set::<Packet>::empty()),
{
}
reason: next_delegate mandates out == empty; if non-empty output is entailed the spec is inconsistent

name: authorized_no_increment
code:
proof fn phi_2_authorized_no_increment(pre: AbstractHostState, post: AbstractHostState, pkt: Packet, out: Set<Packet>)
    requires
        pkt.msg is Message,
        pre.delegation_map.is_complete(),
        next_delegate(pre, post, pkt, out),
        pre.constants.host_ids.contains(pkt.src),
    ensures
        post.num_delegations == pre.num_delegations,
{
}
reason: When source is authorized the counter must increment by 1; staying the same means delegation bookkeeping is lost

name: unauthorized_changes_hashtable
code:
proof fn phi_3_unauthorized_changes_hashtable(pre: AbstractHostState, post: AbstractHostState, pkt: Packet, out: Set<Packet>)
    requires
        pkt.msg is Message,
        pre.delegation_map.is_complete(),
        next_delegate(pre, post, pkt, out),
        !pre.constants.host_ids.contains(pkt.src),
    ensures
        post.h !== pre.h,
{
}
reason: An unauthorized source must not alter the hashtable; entailment here means the spec allows unauthenticated mutation

name: none_value_invalid
code:
proof fn phi_4_none_value_invalid()
    requires
        true,
    ensures
        !valid_optional_value(Option::<AbstractValue>::None),
{
}
reason: None should be a valid optional value; declaring it invalid would reject legitimate absent-value messages

name: update_domain_drops_new_key
code:
proof fn phi_5_update_domain_drops_new_key(h: Hashtable, kr: KeyRange<AbstractKey>, u: Hashtable, k: AbstractKey)
    requires
        u.dom().contains(k),
        kr.contains(k),
    ensures
        !bulk_update_domain(h, kr, u).contains(k),
{
}
reason: A key present in the update map and inside the delegated range must appear in the result; exclusion means delegated data is silently lost
